Brief_Summary: The Surveyor-General of Egypt informs the Director General of the Antiquities Department about the preparation of new maps of Qurna and requests permission to utilize existing contour maps by M. Baraize and access to unpublished sheets 19 and 39 for his reduction.
English_Translation:
18th., February, 1922.

The Director General,
Antiquities Department,
C a i r o.

Sir,

Maps of Qurna.

I have the honour to inform you that in connection
with the 1/1000 map of Qurna which I have recently prepared
at your request, I am also preparing a reduction on a scale
of 1/10,000 for the use of the general public and particularly
to tourists.
As I consider that contours will greatly increase
the usefulness of this map, I am arranging to have these
mapped on the ground, but at the same time I should very much
like to make use of the contours shown on the excellent maps
on a scale of 1/500 of the district prepared by M.Baraize of
your Department.
I am in possession of printed copies of a number of
these sheets and I shall be very grateful if you will permit
me to utilize them in my reduction.
Further, I am informed that sheets Nos.19 and 39
have been completed, although not yet published. If you
will be so good as to arrange for these originals to be sent
me, I will have the necessary reductions made and the originals
returned to you in the course of a few days.
B/-

I have the honour to be,
Sir,
Your obedient servant,

SURVEYOR-GENERAL OF EGYPT.
Confidence_Notes: Some pencil annotations are faint and partially illegible.
Full_Transcription:
18th., February, 1922.

The Director General,
Antiquities Department,
C a i r o.

Sir,

Maps of Qurna.

I have the honour to inform you that in connection
with the 1/1000 map of Qurna which I have recently prepared
at your request, I am also preparing a reduction on a scale
of 1/10,000 for the use of the general public and particularly
to tourists.
As I consider that contours will greatly increase
the usefulness of this map, I am arranging to have these
mapped on the ground, but at the same time I should very much
like to make use of the contours shown on the excellent maps
on a scale of 1/500 of the district prepared by M.Baraize of
your Department.
I am in possession of printed copies of a number of
these sheets and I shall be very grateful if you will permit
me to utilize them in my reduction.
Further, I am informed that sheets Nos.19 and 39
have been completed, although not yet published. If you
will be so good as to arrange for these originals to be sent
me, I will have the necessary reductions made and the originals
returned to you in the course of a few days.
B/-

I have the honour to be,
Sir,
Your obedient servant,

SURVEYOR-GENERAL OF EGYPT.

[Pencil note: faint illegible marks]
[Pencil note: 8.10.22]
[Pencil note: 1922]
